Annual global oil demand growth is often thought to be about 1 million barrels/year.

Today's EIA monthly data indicates the Annual demand growth in the US alone, was just under 600,000 bbls/year.   When you add in the 3rd World, one wonders what it is?#OOTT

➡️ Russia has a shortage of gasoline production, no doubt, but…
➡️ …we see signs that there is a significant logistical aspect to the problem, too. 
➡️ Once the logistical Gordian’s knot is untangled, the shortages at the pump should ease. 
#oott #Russia

➡️ Kazakhstan just doesn’t have the spare refining capacity to move the needle much for Russia. 
➡️ 50ktons/month = 13kbd, while Russia might be 100-150kbd undersupplied right now,
➡️ And if they meant 50ktons total delivered over July and August, divide that in half. 
#oott #Russia

@crudechronicle Oil demand is roughly income elastic. Both the GFC and Covid resulted in parallel shifts downward in these upward-sloping curves. Remains to be seen what the residual impact of this year's drama will be, but absent these events, demand would be noticeably higher.
